events.label: reasoning
events.text: We are asked to assess fall protection equipment with frayed/burned webbing. The work is rough-complete. Reference: OSHA 1926 Subpart M (construction) and 1910.140 (general industry). The condition is frayed/burned webbing. This is a defect. Fall protection equipment must be inspected before use and removed from service if defective. OSHA 1926.502(d)(21) says: "Personal fall arrest systems shall be inspected prior to each use for wear, damage and other deterioration, and defective components shall be removed from service." Also 1910.140(c)(18) similar. Frayed/burned webbing is a clear defect. The work is rough-complete, meaning installed but not closed out. So the equipment is in place but defective. This is non-compliant. Decision: fail. Risk: high or critical? Frayed/burned webbing could fail under load, leading to fall, so critical. Work stage: rough-complete. Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant, degraded (frayed/burned). Percent complete: maybe 90%? But it's rough-complete, so near completion but not accepted. I'll estimate 90. Value: none. Sound source: none. Confidence: high, 0.95. Event time, rate, order, workable: not applicable. Findings: "Frayed/burned webbing on fall protection equipment." Actions: "Immediately remove defective equipment from service and replace with compliant equipment." Rationale: "Frayed/burned webbing compromises the strength of the fall arrest system, violating OSHA 1926.502(d)(21) and 1910.140(c)(18)." References: ["OSHA 1926.502(d)(21)", "1910.140(c)(18)"]. I'll output JSON.

  "actions": ["Immediately remove defective equipment from service and replace with compliant equipment"],
  "rationale": "Frayed or burned webbing compromises the structural integrity of the fall arrest system, violating OSHA 1926.502(d)(21) and 1910.140(c)(18) which require inspection and removal of defective components.",
  "references": ["OSHA 1926.502(d)(21)", "1910.140(c)(18)"]
}
